title: How to fix hooded eyelids with Botox. Brow ptosis vs hooded eyelid.
filename: How to fix hooded eyelids with Botox. Brow ptosis vs hooded eyelid..en.vtt
excerpt: How to treat hooded eyelids this is a really important area to understand and there are two different reasons why your patients might attend if you do not accurately identify these patients you will do the wrong treatment and make them look worse but also miss a potential opportu…

segments.text: how to treat hooded eyelids this is a really important area to understand and there are two different reasons why your patients might attend if you do not accurately identify these patients you will do the wrong treatment and make them look worse but also miss a potential opportunity to make them look better you're going to have patients who have this problem coming to you specifically with the concern but also patients who don't mention it and if you treat them a wrong way you're going to make them very sad tired and unhappy so let's talk about hooded eyelids and what you need to know to inject safely so what exactly are hooded eyelids the way I like to think of it is if the skin from the forehead has almost one continuous plane without any folds interrupting it all the way down to the eyelashes then you have a potential hooded eyelid you can think of this as being most evident if you push down on the eyebrow and you can see the energy making its way all the way through to the eyelid it's actually quite a different structure compared with what many people have for example on my face when I push down on this there is a

an area where there is a divide between the lid and the forehead skin and this prevents my forehead skin from transmitting energy into the eyelid there's also an in between Type where the connections around the ey eyelid skin are not a complete single surface but there's enough energy transmitted from the skin above the eye to rest on the eyelashes themselves and these structures all need to be identified particularly before you do BME toxin in the forehead but also in the patients who are asking you to help lift their eyebrows so one of the reasons why understanding hooded eyelids are so important is because it can very easily confuse a clinician who may think they have an eyelid Tois when in actual fact what they have is a brow tosis this is really important to understand because the cause and the treatment are completely different so this is all about how that energy from the forehead makes its way into the skin near the eyelashes and that's all due to the structure of the eye in this area if you get a droopy eyelid where there's no connection in that energy is likely caused by the levator palpably muscle

being relaxed but if you get the same symptoms but it's to do with loose skin above the eye from the forehead muscle shrinking down you need to do something to lift the eyebrow rather than to treat the levator ppy muscle so usually this is the orbicular Uli relaxing injections that can give you a little lift a brosis is not a hooded eyelid a brosis is a heavy brow but if you have the wrong type of skin you can then get some of that energy resting on your ey and it looks a lot like an eyelid tosis this is usually more of an illusion than reality and if you look very closely at the shape and the position of the eyelashes relative to the iris instead of to the forehead you will see that they are relatively the same maybe with a half millimeter due to the resting but it's the bunching of skin above the eyelashes that makes it look like the eyelid is drooping so let's talk about some different scenarios scenario one would be a patient attending asking to look less tired and you notice that they have hooded eyelids this is a quite a common

situation sometimes they'll be even more specific and say I have hooded eyelids what can you do about it and this is where you will be assessing how much of that hooding is down to the contractility of a bicular ocul muscle so the muscle that pulls the eyebrow down is partially contributing to the position of the hood if you relax that muscle you can lift it a little bit so using the eyebrow Itself by relaxing orbicular oculi and maybe even treating the frontalis you may be able to create a little lift which can open up the eyes slightly so this kind of patient is not particularly risky because they are specifically saying make me look less tired the real tricky ones are the ones who actually have fore headlines and ask you to get rid of forehe headlines and until you notice that getting rid of forehead lines on someone with hooded eyelids causes them to look even worse you'll probably do it at least once maybe twice before you never forget again the treating forehead lines on someone with hooded eyelids is usually not a good idea now there are patients you can get away with it just the right

amount but typically you need to be very careful using toxins with anyone with hooded eyelids unless you're specifically trying to lift their eyebrows I would not be treating only to get rid of forehead lines because so many of them will end up looking tired so how do you manage a patient who comes to you specifically for forehead lines but has significantly hooded eyelids for me it's worth firstly getting to the bottom of why they always attend this is one of the core things I try and get across to people which is most people attend not for a specific reason but to feel a specific way so they want to look younger frh feel more confident all those sorts of things and then I would build the treatment plan around achieving those goals it's then no longer a problem if the treatment will actually degrade their confidence because you'll make them look tireder they'll be grateful for you to tell them that as long as you got some other ideas to help them too now there will be a small number of patients who only want their forehead lines treated they're not interested in anything else and unfortunately you have to think of some other way of treating their forehead lines or not treat them at all but there will be many of them who toxin

is so much of a bad idea that you shouldn't do any at all there'll be more so who you can get away with a little bit uh and you're probably then into the territory where we need to use combination treatments so that could be anything that improves the quality of the skin dermal fillers sometimes um and a little bit of Bim toxin but not too much as to cause a brow drop so it's often a more complex solution because the variables are a bit more Lim lied and you can't do one thing as you would normally without potentially causing a negative outcome so we're doing little tweaks in lots of different areas for a more overall Improvement and most patients who are sensible patients will be grateful for that expert advice if you want more insights and training on bot toxins in particular as well as Anatomy click the link down below I've got something very exciting coming [Music] up [Music]
